mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing market index above 1200 signals a strengthening demand for housing that compresses interest rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 100 signals a weakening supply chain that compresses production costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month increase in the GDP growth rate above 3% signals a monetary-fiscal policy mix that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in monetary-fiscal policy mix periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% and a 2-month increase in the average hourly earnings above 3% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the housing market index above 1200 and a 2-month increase in the retail sales above 500 signals a strengthening demand for housing and retail that compresses interest rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: strengthening demand for housing and retail
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the industrial production index below 100 and a 2-month decline in the GDP growth rate below 2% signals a weakening supply chain and a recession that compresses production costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in weakening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
